body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes — aider has a within-turn reflection loop

The scenario asks for an agent that fails a tool call, interprets the failure, and retries with corrected input across multiple cycles *within one turn* (no intervening user prompt). Aider implements exactly this via its reflection loop. `Coder.run_one` (aider/coders/base_coder.py:924) is a `while message:` loop: it calls `send_message`, and if `self.reflected_message` is set it loops again with that failure text as the next model input, up to `max_reflections = 3` (base_coder.py:100, 939-944). Reflections are seeded by auto-lint failures ("Attempt to fix lint errors?" → `reflected_message = lint_errors`, base_coder.py:1603-1607) and auto-test failures ("Attempt to fix test errors?" → `reflected_message = test_errors`, base_coder.py:1619-1623), and shell-command output is fed back into the conversation as a user/assistant pair (base_coder.py:1609-1614). All of this happens under a SINGLE user prompt: `io.user_input(...)` — which writes the `#### ` history header — is called exactly once per turn, in `run`/`run_stream` BEFORE `run_one` (base_coder.py:860, 879). The reflection iterations inside `run_one` call `send_message` directly and never re-emit a `#### ` header, so the whole fail→retry→recover arc is one logical turn from the transcript's point of view.

### irrlicht_observes: partial — correct topology, but idle-flush timing can split the span

The canonical user-observable signal is: state stays continuously `working` across all retry cycles, with no flicker to `ready` or `waiting` between attempts, ending in `ready`. The aider adapter is structurally well-suited to this. Its `Source` is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", RawLineParser}` (agent.go:45-50). The parser opens a turn on the `#### ` line (`turnOpen = true`) and, critically, treats every `> Tokens: …` line as `assistant_message` — NOT `turn_done` — deliberately keeping the turn open across multiple model calls (parser.go:146-188; the doc comment at parser.go:18-23 calls out that multiple `> Tokens:` lines within one `####` are normal). Tool calls land as `> Running <cmd>` → `tool_result(Bash)` and `> Applied edit to` → `tool_result(Edit)` (parser.go:67-70, 119-124, 231-242), and a `tool_result` never changes session state away from `working`. So the topology the scenario asserts — one contiguous `working` span spanning N tool-call/result pairs and N model calls, then `ready` — is exactly what the parser is built to emit. That is why this is `partial`, not `no`.

The gap that keeps it from a clean `yes` is end-of-turn detection. Aider's idle TUI prompt never lands in the markdown history, so the parser has NO in-band end-of-turn signal; it synthesizes `turn_done` via `IdleFlush` after the transcript has been quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter = 1500ms` (parser.go:54, 208-229). Between two reflection cycles the only thing happening is a model call, and the transcript is silent for the whole duration of that call. If any single inter-reflection model call (LLM latency + any human-confirm pause; aider drives the loop with no `#### ` between cycles) exceeds ~1.5s of file silence, `IdleFlush` fires a premature `turn_done`, the classifier flips `working → ready`, and the next `> Tokens:` line in the *same* logical turn reopens it `ready → working` — a flicker the scenario explicitly forbids. This is the exact failure the matrix note ("idle-flush splits attempts") points at; the root cause is timing, not topology, and it is not always triggered (a fast local model can complete the whole chain inside the window). Hence `partial`: the daemon CAN observe the contiguous span, but cannot GUARANTEE it for arbitrary inter-reflection latencies.

### Primitive / capability anchor

The primitive is `tool_calls` + iterative within-turn retry. The capability key is `tool_calls`, which is `true` in replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json. The within-turn-retry aspect has no dedicated capability flag — it is a property of the classifier holding `working` across `> Tokens:`/`tool_result` runs, not a declared agent feature, so no capabilities.json correction is warranted.

## Matrix comparison

The coverage row already reads `agent_supports: yes / irrlicht_observes: partial`, note "aider retries across turns rather than within; idle-flush splits attempts." This assessment RATIFIES both verdicts. One refinement for the maintainer: the source shows aider's reflection loop is genuinely *within* a single turn (one `#### ` header, `run_one`'s `while message:` loop), so the "retries across turns" half of the note is imprecise — the real and sufficient reason for `partial` is the idle-flush timing window, which the note's second clause already captures.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant adjacent: aider has no in-band end-of-turn marker (the idle TUI prompt is not written to the markdown history), so turn boundaries are inferred purely from a 1500ms idle-flush window (parser.go IdleFlush).

The single forbidden flicker arises when an inter-reflection model call leaves the transcript silent for longer than aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms): IdleFlush synthesizes a premature turn_done (working→ready), then the next reflection's `> Tokens:` line reopens the turn (ready→working). Fast local models that finish each call inside the window avoid this; slow remote models can trip it.

sources.note: FilesUnderCWD markdown parser: `> Tokens:` → assistant_message (turn stays open); `> Running`/`Applied edit` → tool_result; turn_done only via IdleFlush (aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter=1500ms) or LLM-error blockquote
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; per-process Parser instance tracks idle-flush turnOpen state

aider 0.86.2: run_one() while-loop reflection (lines 924-944, max_reflections=3); lint/test failures set reflected_message (1599-1623); io.user_input (the #### header) called once per turn before run_one (run/run_stream, lines 860/879)

user_input() writes the `#### ` prefix to .aider.chat.history.md (lines 775-789); only invoked for the top-level prompt, not for reflected messages — confirms reflections do not re-open a turn in the irrlicht parser
tool_calls: true, headless_mode: true (the two `requires` for this scenario); no within-turn-retry feature flag exists (it is a classifier property)
